message:
fix(security): allow dotted usernames and add TARGET_USER validation gates

Usernames containing dots (e.g. "john.doe") are valid on Linux but were
rejected by the username regex `^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$` used across every
script in the ACFS codebase. This broadens the regex to
`^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$` in all 25+ locations where it appears, spanning
install.sh, every scripts/lib/*.sh module, the manifest generator, the
onboard package, preflight, acfs-global, acfs-update, and
services-setup.

More importantly, this adds explicit validation gates that reject invalid
TARGET_USER values *before* they reach privileged operations like sudo,
useradd, tee-to-sudoers, and shell re-exec. Previously, a malformed
TARGET_USER (e.g. containing path traversal like "../bad") could
propagate unchecked into command arguments. Now:

- install_helpers.sh: new _acfs_valid_target_username() and
  _acfs_validate_target_user() canonical validators; run_as_target()
  calls validate before sudo
- user.sh: new user_valid_target_name() and
  user_require_valid_target_user(); guards added to ensure_user(),
  enable_passwordless_sudo(), migrate_ssh_keys(), set_default_shell()
- services-setup.sh: new services_setup_validate_target_user(); guards
  added to init_target_context(), run_as_user(), run_as_user_shell()
- acfs-global / acfs-update: new validate_target_user_or_die() called
  before main exec_as_target_user and in the re-exec path
- generate.ts: validation injected into both the generated installer
  preamble and the generated doctor checks

Test coverage added:
- test_install_helpers.bats: run_as_target rejects invalid TARGET_USER
  before sudo
- test_user.bats: ensure_user and enable_passwordless_sudo reject
  invalid TARGET_USER before useradd/tee
- test_state.bats: state_resolve_target_home accepts dotted usernames
- test_update.bats: generated installer and doctor checks contain the
  validation calls; wrapper scripts contain updated regex and validation
  gates; username helpers accept dotted usernames across all wrappers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+

@@ -27,7 +27,17 @@ sanitize_abs_nonroot_path() {
 
 is_valid_username() {
     local username="$1"
-    [[ "$username" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]
+    [[ "$username" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]
+}
+
+validate_target_user_or_die() {
+    local user="${1:-}"
+    local display="${user:-<empty>}"
+    if is_valid_username "$user"; then
+        return 0
+    fi
+    echo "Error: invalid TARGET_USER '$display' (expected: lowercase user name like 'ubuntu')" >&2
+    exit 1
 }
 
 resolve_home_for_user() {

-  [[ "$username" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]

+}
+
+validate_target_user_or_die() {
+  local user="${1:-}"
+  local display="${user:-<empty>}"
+  if is_valid_username "$user"; then
+    return 0
+  fi
+  echo "Error: invalid TARGET_USER '$display' (expected: lowercase user name like 'ubuntu')" >&2
+  exit 1
 }

         return 0

@@ -599,6 +599,33 @@ _run_shell_with_strict_mode() {
 }
 
 # Resolve a target user's home via NSS/getent with safe fallbacks.
+if ! declare -f _acfs_valid_target_username >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    _acfs_valid_target_username() {
+        local user="${1:-}"
+        [[ -n "$user" ]] || return 1
+        [[ "$user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]
+    }
+fi
+
+if ! declare -f _acfs_validate_target_user >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    _acfs_validate_target_user() {
+        local user="${1:-${TARGET_USER:-}}"
+        local label="${2:-TARGET_USER}"
+        local display="${user:-<empty>}"
+
+        if _acfs_valid_target_username "$user"; then
+            return 0
+        fi
+
+        if declare -f log_error >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+            log_error "Invalid ${label} '$display' (expected: lowercase user name like 'ubuntu')"
+        else
+            printf "ERROR: Invalid %s '%s' (expected: lowercase user name like 'ubuntu')\n" "$label" "$display" >&2
+        fi
+        return 1
+    }
+fi
+
 if ! declare -f _acfs_resolve_target_home >/dev/null 2>&1; then
     _acfs_resolve_target_home() {
         local user="${1:-ubuntu}"
@@ -640,6 +667,8 @@ if ! declare -f run_as_target >/dev/null 2>&1; then
         local user="${TARGET_USER:-ubuntu}"
         local user_home="${TARGET_HOME:-}"
 
+        _acfs_validate_target_user "$user" "TARGET_USER" || return 1
+
         if [[ -z "$user_home" ]]; then
             user_home="$(_acfs_resolve_target_home "$user" || true)"
         fi

@@ -74,7 +74,7 @@ _lang_target_home() {
         return 0
     fi
 
-    if [[ "$target_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]; then
+    if [[ "$target_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
         printf '/home/%s\n' "$target_user"
         return 0
     fi

@@ -89,7 +89,7 @@ is_fresh_vps() {
             target_home="/root"
         elif [[ "$target_user" == "$(whoami 2>/dev/null || true)" ]] && [[ -n "${HOME:-}" ]] && [[ "${HOME}" == /* ]] && [[ "${HOME}" != "/" ]]; then
             target_home="${HOME%/}"
-        elif [[ "$target_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]; then
+        elif [[ "$target_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
             target_home="/home/$target_user"
         fi
     fi

@@ -51,7 +51,7 @@ _smoke_resolve_current_home() {
             fi
         fi
 
-        if [[ "$current_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]; then
+        if [[ "$current_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then

             return 0
         fi
@@ -87,7 +87,7 @@ if [[ -z "${TARGET_HOME:-}" ]]; then

     elif [[ "${TARGET_USER}" == "$(id -un 2>/dev/null || true)" ]] && [[ -n "${_SMOKE_CURRENT_HOME:-}" ]]; then
         TARGET_HOME="$_SMOKE_CURRENT_HOME"
-    elif [[ "${TARGET_USER}" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]; then
+    elif [[ "${TARGET_USER}" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
         TARGET_HOME="/home/$TARGET_USER"
     fi
     unset _smoke_target_passwd_entry
@@ -97,7 +97,7 @@ if [[ "${TARGET_HOME:-}" != /* ]]; then
         TARGET_HOME="/root"
     elif [[ "${TARGET_USER}" == "$(id -un 2>/dev/null || true)" ]] && [[ -n "${_SMOKE_CURRENT_HOME:-}" ]]; then
         TARGET_HOME="$_SMOKE_CURRENT_HOME"
-    elif [[ "${TARGET_USER}" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]; then
+    elif [[ "${TARGET_USER}" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
         TARGET_HOME="/home/$TARGET_USER"
     else
         TARGET_HOME=""

@@ -127,7 +127,7 @@ _stack_target_home() {
         return 0
     fi
 
-    if [[ "$target_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]; then
+    if [[ "$target_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
         printf '/home/%s\n' "$target_user"
         return 0
     fi

@@ -181,7 +181,7 @@ state_resolve_current_home() {
             fi
         fi
 
-        if [[ "$current_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]; then

             printf '/home/%s\n' "$current_user"
             return 0

             printf '/home/%s\n' "$TARGET_USER"

@@ -60,7 +60,7 @@ _status_resolve_current_home() {
         fi
     fi
 
-    if [[ "$current_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]; then
+    if [[ "$current_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
         printf '/home/%s\n' "$current_user"
         return 0
     fi
@@ -176,7 +176,7 @@ _status_home_for_user() {
         return 0
     fi
 
-    if [[ "$user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]; then
+    if [[ "$user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
         echo "/home/$user"
         return 0
     fi

@@ -46,7 +46,7 @@ support_resolve_current_home() {
         fi
     fi
 
-    if [[ "$current_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]; then
+    if [[ "$current_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
         printf '/home/%s\n' "$current_user"
         return 0

         return 0
     fi
 
-    if [[ "$user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]; then
+    if [[ "$user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
         echo "/home/$user"
         return 0
     fi

@@ -64,7 +64,7 @@ ubuntu_resolve_target_home() {
         return 0
     fi
 
-    if [[ "$target_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]; then
+    if [[ "$target_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
         printf '/home/%s\n' "$target_user"
         return 0
     fi

@@ -1147,7 +1147,7 @@ update_target_home() {
         return 0
     fi
 
-    if [[ "$target_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]; then
+    if [[ "$target_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
         printf '/home/%s\n' "$target_user"
         return 0
     fi

@@ -27,6 +27,23 @@ if ! declare -f log_fatal &>/dev/null; then
     log_step() { printf "[%s] %s\n" "$1" "$2" >&2; }
 fi
 
+user_valid_target_name() {
+    local user="${1:-}"
+    [[ -n "$user" ]] || return 1
+    [[ "$user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]
+}
+
+user_require_valid_target_user() {
+    local user="${1:-${TARGET_USER:-}}"
+    local display="${user:-<empty>}"
+
+    if user_valid_target_name "$user"; then
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    log_fatal "Invalid TARGET_USER '$display' (expected: lowercase user name like 'ubuntu')"
+}
+
 # Ensure SUDO is set (empty string for root, "sudo" otherwise)
 if [[ $EUID -eq 0 ]]; then
     SUDO=""
@@ -59,7 +76,7 @@ user_home_for_user() {
         return 0
     fi
 
-    if [[ "$user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]; then
+    if [[ "$user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
         printf '/home/%s\n' "$user"
         return 0
     fi
@@ -69,6 +86,7 @@ user_home_for_user() {
 
 # Target user for ACFS installations
 TARGET_USER="${TARGET_USER:-ubuntu}"
+user_require_valid_target_user "$TARGET_USER"
 if [[ -z "${TARGET_HOME:-}" ]]; then
     TARGET_HOME="$(user_home_for_user "$TARGET_USER")"
 fi
@@ -103,6 +121,8 @@ _generate_random_password() {
 ensure_user() {
     local target="$TARGET_USER"
 
+    user_require_valid_target_user "$target"
+
     if ! id "$target" &>/dev/null; then
         log_detail "Creating user: $target"
         $SUDO useradd -m -s /bin/bash -G sudo "$target"
@@ -149,6 +169,8 @@ enable_passwordless_sudo() {
     local target="$TARGET_USER"
     local sudoers_file="/etc/sudoers.d/90-ubuntu-acfs"
 
+    user_require_valid_target_user "$target"
+
     log_detail "Enabling passwordless sudo for $target"
 
     echo "$target ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" | $SUDO tee "$sudoers_file" > /dev/null
@@ -171,6 +193,8 @@ migrate_ssh_keys() {
     current_user=$(whoami)
     local target="$TARGET_USER"
 
+    user_require_valid_target_user "$target"
+
     # Nothing to do if we're already the target user
     if [[ "$current_user" == "$target" ]]; then
         log_detail "Already running as $target, no key migration needed"
@@ -318,6 +342,8 @@ set_default_shell() {
     local passwd_entry=""
     local local_entry=""
 
+    user_require_valid_target_user "$target"
+
     if [[ -z "$shell" ]]; then
         shell=$(command -v zsh)
     fi

@@ -49,7 +49,7 @@ services_setup_resolve_current_home() {
             fi
         fi
 
-        if [[ "$current_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*$ ]]; then
+        if [[ "$current_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
             printf '/home/%s\n' "$current_user"
             return 0
         fi
@@ -118,6 +118,24 @@ TARGET_USER="${TARGET_USER:-${SUDO_USER:-$(whoami)}}"
 SERVICES_SETUP_ACTION="${SERVICES_SETUP_ACTION:-}"
 SERVICES_SETUP_NONINTERACTIVE="${SERVICES_SETUP_NONINTERACTIVE:-false}"
 
+services_setup_valid_target_user() {
+    local user="${1:-}"
+    [[ -n "$user" ]] || return 1
+    [[ "$user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]
+}
+
+services_setup_validate_target_user() {
+    local user="${1:-${TARGET_USER:-}}"
+    local display="${user:-<empty>}"
+
+    if services_setup_valid_target_user "$user"; then
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    log_error "Invalid TARGET_USER '$display' (expected: lowercase user name like 'ubuntu')"
+    return 1
+}
+
 resolve_home_dir() {
     local user="$1"
     local home=""
@@ -140,7 +158,7 @@ resolve_home_dir() {
         printf '/root'
         return 0
     fi

@@ -591,6 +591,37 @@ EOF

 }
 
+test_services_setup_rejects_invalid_target_user_before_sudo() {
+    setup_mock_env
+
+    local root_home="$TEST_HOME/root-home"
+    local target_home="$TEST_HOME/target-home"
+    local fake_bin="$TEST_HOME/fake-bin"
+    local sudo_log="$TEST_HOME/sudo.log"
+    local output=""
+
+    mkdir -p "$root_home" "$target_home" "$fake_bin"
+
+    cat > "$fake_bin/sudo" <<EOF
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+printf 'sudo-called\n' >> "$sudo_log"
+exit 0
+EOF
+    chmod +x "$fake_bin/sudo"
+
+    output=$(HOME="$root_home" TARGET_HOME="$target_home" PATH="$fake_bin:/usr/bin:/bin" \
+        bash -c 'source "$1"; TARGET_USER="../bad user"; run_as_user env' _ "$SERVICES_SETUP_SH" 2>&1 || true)
+
+    if [[ "$output" == *"Invalid TARGET_USER '../bad user'"* ]] \
+        && [[ ! -s "$sudo_log" ]]; then
+        harness_pass "services-setup rejects invalid TARGET_USER before sudo"
+    else
+        harness_fail "services-setup rejects invalid TARGET_USER before sudo" "$output"
+    fi
+
+    cleanup_mock_env
+}
+

     setup_mock_env
